We solved this with a clear narrative spine — Vision, Plan, Process, Proof — that carries visitors through the story before asking for a commitment. Social proof, transformations and pricing are all CMS-driven, so the proof grows as the coach does, without touching the layout.

Every decision started from the visitor’s mindset: someone tired of starting over, scanning for a reason to trust. Generous type, calm spacing and a dark, premium palette signal seriousness, while a single, repeated ‘book a call’ action removes any guesswork about the next step.

The template answers the real questions clients ask before committing — how it works, what’s included, whether it fits a busy life — directly in the FAQ and process sections. Nothing is hidden behind a form, which lowers friction and builds confidence before the first email.

Vitalo ships complete: a 12-section homepage, a CMS-powered blog with full article layouts, a transformation gallery, three-tier programs and pricing, a press feature row, contact page, FAQ, and legal pages. Each is a reusable pattern a coach can extend rather than rebuild.

The build is fully responsive across breakpoints, uses semantic headings and considered contrast for readability, and keeps imagery and content in the CMS so pages stay fast and easy to maintain. It’s production-ready the moment it’s remixed.
Vitalo ships as a free, fully responsive, CMS-powered Framer template that’s ready to publish and easy to remix. It gives independent coaches a foundation they can make their own in an afternoon.
